reports.title: The Iran Ceasefire Expires Wednesday and Trump Says He Won't Extend It
reports.subtitle: A 14-day pause in a seven-week war is about to end. The market gives a deal only 17% odds. The blockade of Hormuz is the real fight.

reports.title: Tim Cook Is Out. Apple's New CEO Is a Hardware Engineer in the Age of AI.
reports.subtitle: Apple just handed its future to the person who builds devices, not the person who trains models. That's a deliberate bet on what the AI era actually requires.

reports.title: Kevin Warsh Goes Before the Senate Promising Independence He May Not Be Able to Deliver
reports.subtitle: Trump's Fed pick is vowing to protect central bank autonomy while depending entirely on Trump's party to get confirmed. The contradiction is the point.

reports.title: SCOTUS Takes Up a Case That Could Let Religious Schools Exclude LGBTQ Families and Still Take Public Money
reports.subtitle: The Catholic preschool case is not about preschools. It is about whether publicly funded institutions can use religious identity to opt out of nondiscrimination requirements.

reports.title: Pennsylvania Court Strikes Down Medicaid Abortion Ban, Opening the Next Front in the Post-Dobbs War
reports.subtitle: The ruling uses the state constitution's Equal Rights Amendment to establish a fundamental right to reproductive autonomy. That's a legal argument designed to survive federal courts.

reports.title: China Blocked Nvidia's H200 Chips Even After Trump Gave the Green Light
reports.subtitle: The White House approved the deal. Beijing's customs officials stopped it anyway. The chip war now has two fronts: one in Washington, one inside the US bureaucracy.

reports.title: Virginia Voters Decide Today Whether Democrats Can Gerrymander Back
reports.subtitle: Nearly $100 million spent on a ballot measure that bypasses Virginia's own bipartisan commission. The redistricting wars Trump started now require Democrats to become what they said they were against.

reports.title: China Sent a Carrier Through the Taiwan Strait on the Same Day Japan Did
reports.subtitle: The PLA's Liaoning and Japan's Ikazuchi transited the strait within 24 hours of each other. This is not a coincidence. It is a demonstration of who controls the calendar of escalation.

reports.title: The OECD Says Tariffs Pushed US Inflation to 4.2 Percent. The Market Does Not Believe a Recession Is Coming.
reports.subtitle: Every major forecasting institution is revising US growth down and inflation up, attributing both directly to tariffs. Polymarket still prices a recession at 24.5%. One of them is badly wrong.

reports.title: Trump's EPA Cuts Are Making the Air Dirtier. The People Breathing It Cannot Afford to Move.
reports.subtitle: DOGE-driven EPA staff cuts have gutted enforcement capacity in the same states where air quality was already failing federal standards. The regulatory apparatus still exists on paper. It has stopped functioning.
